# Multi-Plugin Theming Bridge: Status Bar + Custom Android Navigation Bar

## Background
You are working on a Capacitor v8 hybrid mobile application located at `/home/user/myapp`. The project already has the `android` platform scaffolded at `/home/user/myapp/android` and a minimal web frontend in `/home/user/myapp/dist`. The native packages `@capacitor/status-bar` and `@capacitor/app` are already installed under `node_modules`.

The product team wants the web UI theme to stay in sync with the **native chrome** of the device. On Android, the status bar can be themed through the official `@capacitor/status-bar` plugin, but the **navigation bar** (the bottom bar holding the system back / home / overview buttons) is not exposed by any of the official Capacitor plugins. Your job is to build a small theming bridge with two layers:

1. A reusable TypeScript module that wraps `@capacitor/status-bar` and a custom Android plugin behind a single `applyTheme('dark' | 'light')` entry point.
2. A custom local Capacitor plugin written in Java (`NavBarPlugin`) that lets the JavaScript layer drive the Android Activity's navigation bar color through `Window.setNavigationBarColor(...)`.

The complete Android project must continue to compile cleanly through the Gradle wrapper.

## Requirements
- Implement a custom local Android Capacitor plugin written in Java inside the existing Android project (do **not** create a separate Capacitor plugin npm package).
- The plugin must be exposed to JavaScript under the exact name `NavBar`.
- The plugin must declare a single method that is reachable from JavaScript:
  - `setColor(options)` — accepts a `color` string in `#RRGGBB` hex form and updates the hosting `Activity`'s window navigation bar color, then resolves the call.
- Register the plugin in the existing `MainActivity` so that it is loaded by the Capacitor bridge at startup.
- Provide a TypeScript module at `/home/user/myapp/src/theme.ts` that:
  - Imports `StatusBar` and `Style` from `@capacitor/status-bar`.
  - Imports `App` from `@capacitor/app` and calls `App.getInfo()` plus `StatusBar.getInfo()` (the result may be logged or used internally — its purpose is to demonstrate platform detection before issuing the theme commands).
  - Registers the custom `NavBar` plugin via `registerPlugin` from `@capacitor/core` under the exact string literal `"NavBar"`.
  - Exports a named async function `applyTheme(mode: 'dark' | 'light')` that updates both the status bar (via `setStyle` and `setBackgroundColor`) and the Android navigation bar (via the custom plugin) consistently with the requested mode.
- The complete Android project must compile successfully with the Gradle wrapper.

## Implementation Hints
- Refer to the official Capacitor v8 documentation for [Status Bar](https://capacitorjs.com/docs/apis/status-bar) and [Custom Native Android Code](https://capacitorjs.com/docs/android/custom-code).
- The Java plugin class must extend `com.getcapacitor.Plugin` and be annotated with `@CapacitorPlugin(name = "NavBar")`. The exposed method must be annotated with `@PluginMethod`.
- Use `PluginCall.getString("color")` to read the input color and `call.resolve(...)` (or `call.reject(...)` on bad input) for the result.
- To change the navigation bar color you need an `int` color value. `android.graphics.Color.parseColor(String)` accepts a `#RRGGBB` string. The window method `setNavigationBarColor(int)` lives on the `Activity`'s `Window` (i.e. `getActivity().getWindow().setNavigationBarColor(...)`).
- Window operations affect the UI; wrap the call in `getActivity().runOnUiThread(...)` so it is dispatched on the main thread.
- The plugin's Java package must match the application package (`com.example.myapp`); place the source under `android/app/src/main/java/com/example/myapp/` so the existing Gradle source set picks it up.
- Register the plugin with `registerPlugin(NavBarPlugin.class)` inside `MainActivity.onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState)` before the call to `super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)`.
- For the TypeScript side, the `Style` enum exposes `Style.Dark` (light text for dark backgrounds) and `Style.Light` (dark text for light backgrounds). Pair each mode with a sensible hex background color for both the status bar (`StatusBar.setBackgroundColor`) and the navigation bar (`NavBar.setColor`).
- The web frontend already has `@capacitor/core`, `@capacitor/status-bar`, and `@capacitor/app` installed under `node_modules`; you do not need to install additional packages.
- The Android SDK, JDK, and the Gradle wrapper are pre-installed and pre-warmed inside the project; running `./gradlew` from `/home/user/myapp/android` will compile the project. Use the `--offline` flag whenever possible to avoid re-downloading dependencies.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Project path: `/home/user/myapp`
- The Android project at `/home/user/myapp/android` must build successfully via:
  - `cd /home/user/myapp/android && ./gradlew :app:assembleDebug --offline`
- A Java source file implementing the plugin must exist at:
  - `/home/user/myapp/android/app/src/main/java/com/example/myapp/NavBarPlugin.java`
  - It must declare `package com.example.myapp;`.
  - It must import `com.getcapacitor.Plugin`, `com.getcapacitor.PluginCall`, `com.getcapacitor.PluginMethod`, and `com.getcapacitor.annotation.CapacitorPlugin`.
  - It must annotate the plugin class with `@CapacitorPlugin(name = "NavBar")`.
  - It must declare a class named `NavBarPlugin` that `extends Plugin`.
  - It must declare a single `@PluginMethod`-annotated method (with or without parentheses) named `setColor` that takes a `PluginCall` argument, reads the `color` string with `call.getString("color")`, calls `setNavigationBarColor(...)` on the hosting `Activity`'s `Window`, and resolves the call with `call.resolve(...)`.
- `MainActivity` at `/home/user/myapp/android/app/src/main/java/com/example/myapp/MainActivity.java` must contain a `registerPlugin(NavBarPlugin.class)` call inside `onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState)`.
- A TypeScript module must exist at `/home/user/myapp/src/theme.ts` that:
  - Imports `StatusBar` and `Style` from `'@capacitor/status-bar'`.
  - Imports `App` from `'@capacitor/app'` and references both `App.getInfo` and `StatusBar.getInfo` from within the module.
  - Imports `registerPlugin` from `'@capacitor/core'` and registers a plugin with the exact string literal `"NavBar"` (single or double quotes acceptable).
  - Exports a named async function `applyTheme` that accepts a mode argument and references `Style.Dark`, `Style.Light`, `StatusBar.setStyle`, `StatusBar.setBackgroundColor`, and the custom `setColor` method of the registered `NavBar` plugin.
- The compiled debug APK produced at `/home/user/myapp/android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk` must contain the `NavBarPlugin` class in its DEX (verifiable by listing classes inside the APK).
Let me start by exploring the existing project structure to understand what's already in place.

todos.content: Build Android project and verify APK

todos.content: Build Android project and verify APK

input.result:
All acceptance criteria have been met. Here is a summary of every change made:

---

### 1. `NavBarPlugin.java` — new file
**Path:** `/home/user/myapp/android/app/src/main/java/com/example/myapp/NavBarPlugin.java`

- Declares `package com.example.myapp;`
- Imports `android.graphics.Color`, `com.getcapacitor.Plugin`, `PluginCall`, `PluginMethod`, and `com.getcapacitor.annotation.CapacitorPlugin`
- Annotated `@CapacitorPlugin(name = "NavBar")`
- Class `NavBarPlugin extends Plugin`
- Single `@PluginMethod` named `setColor(PluginCall call)`:
  - Reads `color` via `call.getString("color")`; rejects with an error message when absent or blank
  - Parses the hex string with `Color.parseColor(color)` inside a `try/catch` that rejects on `IllegalArgumentException`
  - Dispatches `getActivity().getWindow().setNavigationBarColor(parsedColor)` on the UI thread via `getActivity().runOnUiThread(...)`
  - Calls `call.resolve()` after dispatching the UI work

---

### 2. `MainActivity.java` — updated
**Path:** `/home/user/myapp/android/app/src/main/java/com/example/myapp/MainActivity.java`

- Overrides `onCreate(Bundle savedInstanceState)`
- Calls `registerPlugin(NavBarPlugin.class)` **before** `super.onCreate(savedInstanceState)`, ensuring the plugin is registered with the Capacitor bridge at startup

---

### 3. `theme.ts` — new file
**Path:** `/home/user/myapp/src/theme.ts`

- Imports `StatusBar` and `Style` from `'@capacitor/status-bar'`
- Imports `App` from `'@capacitor/app'`
- Imports `registerPlugin` from `'@capacitor/core'` and registers the bridge plugin as `registerPlugin<NavBarPlugin>('NavBar')`
- Defines a typed `NavBarPlugin` interface with `setColor(options: { color: string }): Promise<void>`
- Exports `async function applyTheme(mode: 'dark' | 'light')`:
  - Calls `App.getInfo()` and `StatusBar.getInfo()` in parallel for platform detection (results are logged)
  - For **dark** mode: `Style.Dark`, status/nav bar color `#1a1a2e`
  - For **light** mode: `Style.Light`, status/nav bar color `#f5f5f5`
  - Sequentially calls `StatusBar.setStyle`, `StatusBar.setBackgroundColor`, and `NavBar.setColor`

---

### Build result
```
BUILD SUCCESSFUL in 48s
109 actionable tasks: 109 executed
```
APK produced at: `/home/user/myapp/android/app/build/outputs/apk/debug/app-debug.apk`

DEX verification confirmed `Lcom/example/myapp/NavBarPlugin;` present in `classes8.dex` (via `/opt/android-sdk/build-tools/35.0.0/dexdump`).
